textPlain: It’s hard to control the narrative when people can say what they want on the internet, anonymously, without being punished for it.

The UK government is facing calls to restrict children’s access to VPNs.VPNs aren't capable of harming kids. They aren't content and even the usual imagined harms can't be caused by a VPN.The Gov's entire argument seems to be 'We aren't getting our way".Imagine if Gov officials cared about Gov officials trading law for favors - as much as they do about popular hysteria.

The money quote is that it's not at all clear whether the sudden VPN usage is from kids or from adults to (I think rightfully) don't want to hand over their government id or their CC to random websites.Because yeah, fuck that shit. The presumption should be that all content on the internet is for adults. If you want your site to be child friendly you can opt into a higher standard of moderation. How did we go from the internet is for porn to this?!You can't get an internet connection if you aren't an adult. There is always an adult giving the child access who is in a position to apply filtering. From your house to schools to libraries access is mediated by an adult, use that.
VPNs were the target from the beginning. Whenever a politician says "think of the children," it's the beginning of the wedge strategy.
Lawmakers making laws about technical matters don't realize that they don't understand those technical matters well enough to make intelligent laws about them.Shocking! Unprecedented! Film at 11!
This honestly so pathetic, you have to consider that bongs arent even people anymore.
Why do you want people punished for saying things?I guess maybe this was sarcasm. If so, carry on good sir.
